search:nextjs+typescript-eslint:nextjs t.content:
- Use `pnpm dlx eslint --init` to set up ESLint.
- Choose "React" and "TypeScript" when prompted during initialization.
- Install shared configurations with `eslint:recommended` and `@typescript-eslint/recommended`.
- Configure Prettier integration by installing `eslint-config-prettier` and `eslint-plugin-prettier`, then adding them to your `.eslintrc.json`.
- Use the latest versions of ESLint, TypeScript, and Prettier.
- Key config options include `"extends": ["eslint:recommended", "plugin:@typescript-eslint/recommended"], "plugins": ["@typescript-eslint", "prettier"], "rules": {"prettier/prettier": "error"}`.

- Install ESLint and TypeScript ESLint plugins via npm or yarn.
- Use `npx eslint --init` to create an ESLint configuration file.
- Select "React" and "TypeScript" when prompted during initialization.
- Add `"@typescript-eslint/parser"` as the parser in your `.eslintrc.json`.
- Include `"@next/eslint-plugin-next"` for Next.js specific linting rules.
- Set `"extends": ["eslint:recommended", "plugin:@typescript-eslint/recommended", "plugin:@next/eslint-plugin-next/recommended"]` to inherit recommended rules.
- Configure `rules` in `.eslintrc.json` to override or extend default settings as needed.
- Run `npx eslint --fix .` to automatically fix linting issues.
- Integrate ESLint with your editor for real-time feedback and auto-fix capabilities.
